completion_criteria_checked: docs/BRR_POLICY.md gains a 'Self-Verification Restrictions' section defining which task classes/types may self-verify, which require a second reviewer or stronger checks, and what extra evidence is required. -- MET: read the full added section directly from the file; class-by-class rules (A/B/C/D), a named circularity restriction requiring a real second reviewer or BLOCKED rather than self-close for a defined narrow category, and four explicit extra-evidence items are all present.

The section is explicit that it bounds/tightens DECISION-033/036 rather than redefining, expanding, or replacing that authority grant. -- MET: the section opens by stating this directly, and the 'Notes on scope' subsection repeats it; re-read DECISION-033/036's full text this cycle and confirmed neither was altered (git diff confirms docs/DECISIONS.md's only change is the new DECISION-047 entry appended after DECISION-046, no edits to existing decision text).

The section reconciles with, and does not contradict, Task Safety Classification, Acceptance Boundary Rules, and the Verification Depth Policy. -- MET: re-read all three sections directly; the circularity restriction is additive on top of the existing strict-depth requirement (Verification Depth Policy) rather than replacing it, and the class-by-class rules restate rather than contradict the Acceptance Boundary Rules table.
A new decision is recorded in docs/DECISIONS.md. -- MET: DECISION-047 present, correctly cross-referenced.

No runtime script, schema, or enforcement change; DECISION-033/036's wording unaltered; no Phase 2 autonomy decision reopened. -- MET: git diff confined to docs/BRR_POLICY.md, docs/DECISIONS.md (additive only), and the live state/handoff/result files; no file under scripts/ or schemas/ touched; DECISION-038 through 042 and 045 text unchanged.

No existing verdict, task safety class, or stop condition is redefined or weakened. -- MET: the new section explicitly reuses Stop-Instead-of-Guess trigger 6 (BLOCKED) rather than inventing a new verdict; spot-checked, no redefinition found.

risks: Self-verified under DECISION-033 degraded fallback (Codex unavailable). No independent second-party (Codex) review occurred. GPT secondary review: GPT provided the recommendation that prompted this task's self-promotion, but did not review the completed result itself this cycle -- that is a distinct, narrower role than the review disclosure this line normally covers, and is named explicitly rather than conflated with a completed-result review.

Verification Depth Policy row applied: Class B, truth-surface-affecting -> strict. Strict depth was applied (full read of the added section plus line-by-line cross-check against Task Safety Classification, Acceptance Boundary Rules, and Verification Depth Policy, performed and recorded here, not merely asserted).

Circularity-restriction self-check: this task does NOT fall into the new circularity case it defines (it does not modify DECISION-033/036's text, the autonomous gate scripts, or a Task Safety Class's core definition -- it adds a new adjacent policy section, the same shape as pcc-brr3-001). It was therefore correctly eligible for self-verification under this cycle's own new rule, not an exception to it.

This is the first self-promoted task since BRR Phase 2 (promotion_basis populated). The promotion reasoning was independently re-derived against the actual 8-part gate rather than accepted on GPT's recommendation, but it remains PCC's own self-assessment of its own self-promotion -- exactly the kind of call a future independent reviewer (Codex, or a more adversarial GPT pass with the actual diff) should re-check rather than assume correct because it is thoroughly documented.

The new circularity restriction is definitional and judgment-applied only; nothing currently detects automatically whether a future diff touches the gate scripts or a class definition -- a verifier has to notice this by reading the diff, same limitation disclosed for Verification Depth Policy's own enforcement gap.

This cycle's changes are committed locally per the standing DECISION-020 default; pushing to any remote requires a fresh, explicit owner approval this cycle.
summary: Verified pcc-brr3-002 (BRR Policy: Self-Verification Limits) at 'strict' depth, as this task's own predecessor policy (pcc-brr3-001's Verification Depth Policy) requires for a Class B, truth-surface-affecting change. All six completion criteria are met with direct evidence. Independent local guardrails clean. This is the first self-promoted task since Phase 2: PCC independently re-walked the full 8-part Safe Next-Task Drafting Rules gate rather than accepting GPT's (today's advisor/secondary-reviewer) recommendation as authority, per DECISION-036's own limit on GPT's role -- the recorded promotion_basis reflects that independent check. The new section's circularity restriction was also applied to this task itself and correctly found not to trigger self-closure ineligibility, since the task adds an adjacent policy section rather than modifying the self-verification/gate/classification mechanisms it restricts.
